trial_details.eval_reason: Pod is Running with all containers ready (1/1)
trial_details.trajectory:
I'll diagnose and fix the Kubernetes pod "volume-mount-app" in namespace "case7-test". Let me start by gathering information about the pod.Perfect! I've identified the issue. The pod is trying to mount a ConfigMap named "app-configuration" that doesn't exist. Let me check what ConfigMaps are available in the namespace and then create the missing one.The ConfigMap "app-configuration" is missing. Let me create it:Perfect! The ConfigMap has been created. The pod should now be able to mount the volume and start successfully.

## DIAGNOSIS
The pod "volume-mount-app" was stuck in "ContainerCreating" state because it was trying to mount a ConfigMap volume named "app-configuration" that did not exist in the namespace. The kubelet was repeatedly failing to mount the volume with the error "configmap 'app-configuration' not found".

## FIX APPLIED
Created the missing ConfigMap "app-configuration" in the case7-test namespace with the command:
```
kubectl create configmap app-configuration -n case7-test --from-literal=config.txt="default configuration"
```

This allows the pod to successfully mount the required ConfigMap volume and proceed with startup.

**FIX COMPLETE**

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:
1. $ kubectl get pod volume-mount-app -n case7-test
   Output: NAME               READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE
volume-mount-app   0/1     ContainerCreating   0          13s...
2. $ kubectl describe pod volume-mount-app -n case7-test
   Output: Name:             volume-mount-app
Namespace:        case7-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Thu, 25 Dec 2025 10:49:43 +0300
...
3. $ kubectl get configmap -n case7-test
   Output: NAME               DATA   AGE
kube-root-ca.crt   1      22s...
4. $ kubectl create configmap app-configuration -n case7-test --from-literal=config.txt="default configuration"
   Output: configmap/app-configuration created...
